title: Github 1521 Inventory And Reduce Slow Or Redundant Remaining

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Start #1521 with a current test inventory and one concrete low-risk suite reduction.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not weaken lifecycle, startup/preflight, proof, report, closeout, package install, or generated target freshness coverage.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Choose a bounded reduction slice when equivalent assertions remain and validation can prove the preserved behavior.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.
canonical_core.next_action: Continue with the next planned reduction slice after the #1525 inventory refresh.
proof_expectations: Packaging test subset passes with duration output.
Maintainer surface guard for testing strategy passes.
Workspace summary and implement proof selection pass after planning updates.
completion_criteria: Current root and package test counts are recorded.
Repeated packaging wheel/sdist builds are consolidated without removing artifact, install, or workflow assertions.
#1522 planning candidate is closed as covered by this slice.
#1523 stable generated-output assertions are removed from ordinary proof-runner smoke coverage.
#1524 active-only and verbose preflight mode checks are merged into one scenario matrix with before/after evidence.
#1525 testing-strategy and replacement inventories are refreshed against current counts and guard coverage.
Remaining #1521 work is left open with the next issue in priority order.

goal: Open upstream issue from refreshed external intent evidence; priority P1 inferred from inferred-lane.
non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Packaging artifact tests now separate artifact build cost from assertion count, generated-command proof-runner smoke coverage no longer reasserts selected generated output values already owned by conformance, live-checkout preflight modes are grouped as one scenario matrix, and maintainer inventory docs now match current counts and conformance surfaces.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Current inventory documented, packaging tests now reuse module-scoped built artifacts, proof-runner smoke no longer duplicates two conformance-owned output field checks, preflight active/full mode checks are one scenario matrix, and maintainer inventories reflect current counts/conformance surfaces.

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.
closure_check.why this decision is honest: #1521 requires more reduction slices; this change completes the inventory refresh, #1522 packaging-build reduction, bounded #1523 proof-runner assertion pruning, bounded #1524 preflight mode consolidation, and bounded #1525 inventory/guard refresh.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Proof commands passed for the packaging, #1523, #1524, and #1525 slices, including expanded implement-selected proof; #1526 remains the next priority candidate.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Packaging tests rebuild duplicate artifacts per assertion, inventory counts drift without documentation, proof-runner smoke starts asserting conformance-owned generated output values again, preflight mode tests split duplicate live-checkout setup again, or maintainer docs preserve stale counts/conformance assumptions.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, report AW smoothness/helpfulness gaps, better-way signals, unused-feature reflections, and places AW could help more. Route each concrete signal to exactly one owner class unless explicitly split, or mark no_signal_found after checking.
